# Questions in Livestream 126

Live Stream #126: Apocalypse Of The Sock Puppet

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Ou2jBECn4rM?t=886" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">14:46</a> | As a sci-fi fan, what does he think of Grand Admiral Thrawn's return to Star Wars? | He says he knows the character but had not watched enough of the new material to give a strong verdict. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Ou2jBECn4rM?t=1049" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">17:29</a> | In Judges 19:1-25, why did the Levite force his two women out, and what is the cultural context for offering daughters to protect a guest? | He treats the passage as horrifying and explains it through ancient hospitality, social disorder, and the text's own moral indictment. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Ou2jBECn4rM?t=1869" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">31:09</a> | In Judges 19:29, did the Levite lie to Israel by sending pieces of the woman and making it seem like the Benjaminites killed her? | He says yes, the act was a manipulative way to spark outrage. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Ou2jBECn4rM?t=2225" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">37:05</a> | Why does God not let Israel win immediately in Judges 20:20-28, and does that imply divine willingness for defeat? | He treats the passage as a judgment and testing episode rather than a denial of God's sovereignty or concern. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Ou2jBECn4rM?t=2780" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">46:20</a> | Why did God kill David's son, and why was the child part of the judgment against David's sin? | He says the judgment had to wound David and works through the episode as serious covenant discipline. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Ou2jBECn4rM?t=3115" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">51:55</a> | What specific updates would Kenneth Kitchen's book on the reliability of the Old Testament need? | He says most of the book is still useful, but many discoveries since publication would need to be added. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Ou2jBECn4rM?t=3270" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">54:30</a> | What is his take on John Walton's view of Genesis 1 as a cosmic temple dedication text? | He largely agrees with Walton on Genesis 1 as sacred-space and temple language, while disagreeing on the serpent. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Ou2jBECn4rM?t=3411" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">56:51</a> | What happened to his project on the plagues of Egypt? | It has not been abandoned, but time and funding limits have delayed it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Ou2jBECn4rM?t=3751" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:02:31</a> | What does Ecclesiastes 12:12 mean, and is it condemning great study? | No. He says it warns that excessive study is wearying without condemning scholarship. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Ou2jBECn4rM?t=4162" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:09:22</a> | What did obelisks represent in Egyptian culture and religion, and do they imitate alien technology? | Obelisks are tied to sun worship and temple symbolism; he rejects the alien-technology idea. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Ou2jBECn4rM?t=4349" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:12:29</a> | How accurate are ancient population estimates for Israel? | He says they are still estimates with the same basic uncertainty problems that affect other ancient demographic reconstructions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Ou2jBECn4rM?t=4957" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:22:37</a> | What books, tools, and sources does he recommend for going deep into ancient chronology? | He points to source texts and specialized chronological work, while noting that the field is difficult and needs careful method. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Ou2jBECn4rM?t=5355" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:29:15</a> | How does he understand Esther 2:5-7 and the question of who was taken captive? | He reads the grammar as referring to Mordecai's family line rather than requiring Mordecai himself to be impossibly old. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Ou2jBECn4rM?t=6196" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:43:16</a> | Were randomized methods considered legitimate for inquiring of the Lord in Proverbs 16:33, and are they still valid? | He explains the lot as a biblical practice but does not treat casual modern randomization as a simple equivalent. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Ou2jBECn4rM?t=6481" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:48:01</a> | What view does he take on Pentateuchal authorship? | He favors a conservative supplementary-style approach over more radical source fragmentation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Ou2jBECn4rM?t=6633" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:50:33</a> | Are there books or scholars that defend the supplementary view he described? | He points to peer-reviewed work and says the view appears more often in European scholarship. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Ou2jBECn4rM?t=6720" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:52:00</a> | Is the Bible better understood primarily as poetic work rather than straightforward story? | He says poetry matters deeply, but he would not push that framing so far that the Bible's historical claims disappear. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Ou2jBECn4rM?t=7524" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:05:24</a> | Does he use Logos Bible Software? | No. He says he uses BibleWorks and physical books instead. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Ou2jBECn4rM?t=7678" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:07:58</a> | What can Christians do to combat anti-Semitism in the church? | He says Christians should remember Romans, reject supersessionist hostility, and support Jewish people rightly. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Ou2jBECn4rM?t=8321" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:18:41</a> | Does Christ still have a body in heaven? | Yes. He says Christ remains the resurrected God-man. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Ou2jBECn4rM?t=9056" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:30:56</a> | How do Old Testament apostasy laws differ from Islamic penalties for leaving Islam? | He says the systems are different and that ancient Israel usually did not execute someone merely for becoming apostate. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Ou2jBECn4rM?t=9929" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:45:29</a> | Why do Egyptian loanwords drop off so sharply after the Exodus materials? | He says the shift reflects changing language environment and setting once Israel is no longer embedded in Egypt. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Ou2jBECn4rM?t=10784" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:59:44</a> | What does he think of the Septuagint versus the Masoretic Text? | He prefers the Masoretic Text as a reading edition but uses text criticism case by case, including the Septuagint and other witnesses. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Ou2jBECn4rM?t=11548" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:12:28</a> | What does he think about Trinitarian theology, modalism, divine simplicity, and the filioque? | He rejects modalism as non-Trinitarian and says he does not support the filioque. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Ou2jBECn4rM?t=12075" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:21:15</a> | Who has the worse Egyptian chronology, David Rohl or Roger Pearlman? | He says he cannot assess Pearlman, but he can say Rohl's chronology is very bad. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Ou2jBECn4rM?t=12856" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:34:16</a> | Could a similar chronological system be used to date events in the Bible? | Yes, but it would need to be adapted carefully for clans and biblical genealogical data. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Ou2jBECn4rM?t=13111" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:38:31</a> | Why did Moses break the Ten Commandment tablets? | He says the act symbolized that the covenant had already been broken before it was even formally ratified. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Ou2jBECn4rM?t=13891" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:51:31</a> | Did the three-fish painting behind him come with the apartment, or is it his own work? | He says it is three poppies, not three fish, and it is not presented as his major subject. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Ou2jBECn4rM?t=14131" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:55:31</a> | Does a beginner need preparatory study before reading his Ark of the Covenant book? | No. The book was written for a lay audience rather than only for specialists. |
